title: Dennstaedtia gomphophylla
taxonomy: Dennstaedtia gomphophylla is a species of fern in the family Dennstaedtiaceae. It belongs to the class Polypodiopsida, which is a group of vascular plants that have a fern-like life cycle. The class Polypodiopsida is further divided into several orders, with Dennstaedtia gomphophylla being part of the order Polypodiales. This order is characterized by the presence of vascular tissue and the production of spores on the underside of the fronds. The family Dennstaedtiaceae is a group of ferns that are commonly known as the "basket ferns".

intro: Dennstaedtia gomphophylla, also known as the tree fern Dennstaedtia, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dennstaedtiaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, contributing to its use in treating various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Dennstaedtia gomphophylla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Dennstaedtia gomphophylla is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to make ointments and salves for topical applications.
